skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos through the inference.sh belt CLI using models for text-to-video, image-to-video, reference-to-video, lipsync, editing, upscaling, foley, and media merging.
skill.summary: Generate and edit AI videos with inference.sh video models from Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.
skill.author: inference-skills

security_audit.summary: Static external command and network findings are mostly true positives because the skill is built around belt CLI commands that call remote inference.sh video apps. No malicious exfiltration, prompt injection, or weak cryptographic implementation was found; the weak crypto findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 72 are false positives from documentation text.

medium_findings.title: External AI CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill grants scoped Bash access to belt and provides many belt app run examples. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it executes a local CLI that contacts external inference.sh services and may process user prompts, images, audio, or video URLs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool and repeated belt app run examples confirm real external command usage. The commands match the stated AI video purpose, so the concern is operational risk rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Remote Media URL Processing
medium_findings.description: Several examples pass image_url, audio_url, video_url, and video arrays to remote model apps. Users could expose private media URLs or sensitive prompts to third-party inference services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples directly include media URL fields sent through belt app run inputs. The URLs are placeholders, but the workflow clearly relies on external media processing.

medium_findings.title: Related Skill Installation Commands
medium_findings.description: The related skills section suggests npx skills add commands for additional skills. These are documentation examples, but following them expands the user environment and creates supply chain review requirements.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are present in a markdown code block and are not covered by the skill allowed-tools entry. They are still actionable installation instructions that users may copy.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and Placeholder URLs
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to inference.sh pages, an image asset, documentation, install instructions, or placeholder media examples. No evidence found that these URLs exfiltrate secrets or call an unrelated endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links and placeholder inputs in markdown. They are network indicators, but their context is expected for a CLI integration skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm False Positive
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography hits at the description and table header locations do not contain cryptographic code, hashing APIs, encryption routines, or security decisions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of both cited lines shows plain markdown metadata and a table header. There is no implementation of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any other cryptographic algorithm.

dangerous_patterns.title: Scoped Shell Permission for belt
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill permits Bash commands matching belt, which can run external model jobs through the inference.sh CLI.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration is explicit and directly authorizes belt command execution. The scope is limited to belt rather than arbitrary shell commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: belt app run with User Inputs
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples send prompts and media URL fields through belt app run inputs to hosted AI video apps.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern and input fields are repeated across concrete examples. This confirms intentional remote processing of user-provided content.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Skill Installation Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The related skills section includes npx skills add examples that could install additional community skill content if copied by a user.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are static documentation examples, not automatically executed. They remain a real supply chain pattern because they instruct installation of additional packages.

content.value_statement: Creating AI video often requires many model-specific commands and input formats. This skill provides ready CLI patterns for video generation, animation, editing, lipsync, foley, and upscaling.
actual_capabilities: Lists inference.sh video models and app IDs for text-to-video, image-to-video, and reference-to-video tasks.
Provides belt CLI examples for Veo, Grok, Wan, Seedance, HappyHorse, and other video models.
Shows avatar and lipsync workflows using image and audio URL inputs.
Includes examples for video editing, foley sound generation, upscaling, and media merging.
Links to inference.sh documentation for running apps, streaming results, and content pipelines.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh belt CLI and an authenticated account before commands can run.
Uses external hosted AI services, so prompts and media URLs may leave the local environment.
Does not provide current model pricing, rate limits, content policy details, or availability guarantees.
Example media URLs are placeholders and must be replaced with accessible user-owned files.

use_cases.title: Create Short Marketing Clips
use_cases.description: Generate prompt-based product scenes, social posts, and campaign visuals with model-specific belt commands.

use_cases.title: Animate Existing Creative Assets
use_cases.description: Turn still images, reference portraits, or brand visuals into motion clips using image-to-video and reference-to-video models.

use_cases.title: Prototype Video Production Pipelines
use_cases.description: Combine generation, editing, lipsync, foley, upscaling, and merging commands into repeatable AI video workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Basic Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a short AI video from this idea: [scene]. Use an inference.sh model that fits quality and speed needs.

prompt_templates.title: Animate an Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Image-to-video with a supplied image URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this image into a [duration]-second video: [image URL]. Make the motion [motion style] and keep the subject recognizable.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Talking Head
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking-head video using this portrait [image URL] and this audio [audio URL]. Choose a lipsync model and explain the command.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Video Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a full AI video workflow for [goal]. Include generation, editing, sound, upscaling, and final merge steps with suitable inference.sh models.

output_examples.input: Generate a five-second video of a city skyline at sunrise.
output_examples.output: A recommended text-to-video model, a belt command outline, and notes about duration, prompt wording, and optional audio.

output_examples.input: Animate a product image and add sound effects.
output_examples.output: A two-step workflow that first creates motion from the image, then adds foley audio using a video URL input.

output_examples.input: Make a portrait speak from an uploaded audio file.
output_examples.output: A lipsync or avatar model recommendation with required image and audio URL fields and privacy reminders.

best_practices: Use non-sensitive prompts and media URLs unless the inference.sh account and model terms allow private content.
Start with fast models for drafts, then rerun final prompts on higher quality models when needed.
Keep source images, audio, and video files organized so generated outputs can be traced to their inputs.
anti_patterns: Do not paste private customer media or confidential scripts into remote model inputs without approval.
Do not assume every listed model has the same duration, resolution, cost, or audio support.
Do not install related skills from examples without reviewing their source and permissions first.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create and edit AI videos using inference.sh CLI commands for several hosted video models.

faq.question: Does it require an external service?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples use the inference.sh belt CLI and hosted model apps, so network access and login are required.

faq.question: Can it animate existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes image-to-video examples for Wan, Seedance, HappyHorse, and related models.

faq.question: Can it create lipsync or avatar videos?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists OmniHuman, Fabric, and PixVerse lipsync workflows that use image and audio inputs.

faq.question: Does it include pricing or current model availability?
faq.answer: No. Users should verify current pricing, quotas, limits, and availability in inference.sh before production use.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with private media?
faq.answer: Use caution. The workflow sends prompts and media URLs to remote services, so private content needs policy review first.
